module-1-field-notes-repository.label: Project: Build the Field Notes Repository
module-1-field-notes-repository.summary: Carry one garden-notes project from an ordinary folder to a clean first snapshot, using the same inspect–act–verify rhythm from the module.

sketch0.title: The Garden Notes Need a Beginning

try_git_repo_inspection.title: Initialize the Field Notes Repository
try_git_repo_inspection.prompt:
You are already inside the prepared `garden-notes/` project folder. It contains field-note files but is not a Git repository yet.

Initialize this folder with `main` as the first branch. Do not change directories; the terminal starts in the correct folder.
try_git_repo_inspection.hint: Create the repository in the current folder and choose the requested first branch at the same time.

help.concept: Initialization creates repository metadata inside the current folder; it does not stage files or create a commit.
help.hint_1: The terminal is already inside `garden-notes/`, so the repository should be created here.
help.hint_2: Use the initialization option that names `main` as the first branch.

try_git_repo_inspection.instructions:
You are already inside the prepared `garden-notes/` project folder. It contains field-note files but is not a Git repository yet.

Initialize this folder with `main` as the first branch. Do not change directories; the terminal starts in the correct folder.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf garden-notes
mkdir -p garden-notes/observations
printf '# Garden Field Notes\n' > garden-notes/README.md
printf 'Plot A: soil is damp after rain.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-a.txt
printf 'Plot B: seedlings need labels.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-b.txt

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu

printf 'Plot B: seedlings need labels.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-b.txt

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you initialize `garden-notes/` with `main` as its first branch?

try_git_config.title: Set the Repository Identity
try_git_config.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

The repository now exists. Set `user.name` to `ZoeSkoul Learner` and `user.email` to `learner@zoeskoul.local` for this repository only, then print both local values.
try_git_config.hint: Keep the author identity inside this repository rather than changing machine-wide settings.

help.concept: Repository-local configuration gives commits an author without changing machine-wide Git settings.
help.hint_1: Both the name and email need repository-local values.
help.hint_2: Read the stored values back to catch spelling or scope mistakes.

try_git_config.solutionCode:
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git config --local --get user.name
git config --local --get user.email
try_git_config.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

The repository now exists. Set `user.name` to `ZoeSkoul Learner` and `user.email` to `learner@zoeskoul.local` for this repository only, then print both local values.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf garden-notes
mkdir -p garden-notes/observations
printf '# Garden Field Notes\n' > garden-notes/README.md
printf 'Plot A: soil is damp after rain.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-a.txt
printf 'Plot B: seedlings need labels.\n' > garden-notes/observations/plot-b.txt
git -C garden-notes init -q -b main

try_git_init.title: Read the Untracked Project Files
try_git_init.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

The identity is ready, but Git is not tracking the notes yet. Use short status and identify the README plus both observation files as untracked.
try_git_init.hint: Use the compact repository report and read both status columns.

help.concept: Carry one garden-notes project from an ordinary folder to a clean first snapshot, using the same inspect–act–verify rhythm from the module.
help.hint_1: Focus on the path named in the task and decide whether it is untracked, staged, or modified.
help.hint_2: Compare the final status with the exact state the prompt asks you to prove.

try_git_init.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

The identity is ready, but Git is not tracking the notes yet. Use short status and identify the README plus both observation files as untracked.

try_git_stage_files.title: Stage the Field Notes
try_git_stage_files.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

Prepare the first snapshot by staging `README.md` and everything under `observations/`. Then list the staged file names so you can verify the boundary before committing.
try_git_stage_files.hint: Choose only the paths that belong to this checkpoint.

help.concept: A commit records only the staged snapshot and gives it a message another reader can understand.
help.hint_1: Do not broaden the staging boundary just to make status quieter.
help.hint_2: Review the staged paths or patch before moving on.

try_git_stage_files.solutionCode:
git add README.md observations/
git diff --staged --name-only
try_git_stage_files.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

Prepare the first snapshot by staging `README.md` and everything under `observations/`. Then list the staged file names so you can verify the boundary before committing.

try_git_commit.title: Create the First Snapshot
try_git_commit.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

The three project files are staged. Commit them with the message `Start garden field notes`, read the one-line history, and finish by checking that the working tree is clean.
try_git_commit.hint: Record only the staged snapshot and use the message requested by the scenario.

help.concept: Log gives the timeline overview; show opens the contents of one recorded snapshot.
help.hint_1: The message should describe the project result, not the commands you used.
help.hint_2: After recording it, inspect history and any work that remains.

try_git_commit.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Start garden field notes"
git log --oneline
git status --short
try_git_commit.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `garden-notes/` repository.

The three project files are staged. Commit them with the message `Start garden field notes`, read the one-line history, and finish by checking that the working tree is clean.

moduleProject.title: Build the Field Notes Repository
moduleProject.description:
The garden team has three useful files but no trustworthy beginning. Build the repository in five cumulative milestones: initialize on `main`, keep the author identity local, inspect the untracked set, select the first snapshot, and record it.

Finish with one commit named `Start garden field notes`, all three project files tracked, and a clean short-status result.

intro-to-git-repository.title: A Field Notebook Becomes a Repository
intro-to-git-repository.bodyMarkdown:
The neighborhood garden has useful observations, but the folder has no dependable beginning. A new volunteer can see today’s files yet cannot tell when the notebook started or which version should be trusted.

You will build that beginning in five milestones: initialize the folder, set a local identity, inspect the untracked notes, stage the intended project files, and record one first snapshot. Each milestone begins from the completed state of the previous one, so the project remains cumulative without exposing platform setup.

The final repository should contain one commit named `Start garden field notes` and no unfinished changes.
